open_findings.title: Follow creation increments profile counters twice
open_findings.description: FollowsService.followUser increments follower/following profile counters inside the transaction, then immediately performs a second updateMany increment after the transaction. A successful follow can therefore add 2 to each denormalized counter for a single Follow row.
open_findings.impact: Follower/following counts drift upward and user profiles show inflated social proof; any ranking or recommendations based on these counters become unreliable.

proof_hooks.summary: Transaction increments both profile counters before returning the created Follow row.
A second updateMany block increments the same follower/following counters again.

suggested_fix.approach: Remove the post-transaction updateMany block or replace the transaction updates with updateMany, but keep exactly one counter increment per successful Follow create.
tests_needed: Add or run a follow/unfollow service test that asserts counters change by exactly one.

history.notes: Created during LYRA audit pass.
Removed duplicate post-transaction profile counter updates in follows.service followUser/unfollowUser.

open_findings.title: Music phase-2 vertical is exposed in production UI without a feature flag
open_findings.description: The project expectations state that the Music phase-2 vertical must not be accessible or visible in production UI until backend models are complete. The web app includes public /music routes, an onboarding link to /music, and Music copy in product pages without an observed production feature flag gate.
open_findings.impact: Production users can enter an incomplete vertical, creating broken or misleading product flows and violating a critical launch constraint.

proof_hooks.summary: /music renders MusicDiscoveryPage and exposes upload and artist-profile CTAs.
Onboarding includes a Music step that links to /music.
Expectation 8.1 forbids exposing Music phase-2 in production without complete backend support.

suggested_fix.approach: Gate all Music routes and navigation entries behind a production-safe feature flag until the vertical is explicitly ready, or document backend completeness and update the expectation.
tests_needed: Build web with the flag disabled and verify /music is not reachable from navigation/onboarding.

history.notes: Created during LYRA audit pass.
Music phase-2 gated via isMusicPhase2Enabled / NEXT_PUBLIC_FEATURE_MUSIC_PHASE2; nav, onboarding, composer, and index page.

open_findings.title: CI and deploy web builds omit required NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL
open_findings.description: apps/web/next.config.js now throws when NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL is missing, but the CI Build Web step only sets NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL and the deploy Build Web step sets neither public URL. In a clean GitHub Actions environment without apps/web/.env.local, next build will fail before deployment artifacts are produced.
open_findings.impact: Main-branch deploys can be blocked by environment validation even when application code compiles locally with a checked-in or local .env file.

proof_hooks.summary: next.config.js requires NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL during build/start.
CI Build Web exports NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL but not NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL.
Deploy Build Web runs npm --prefix apps/web run build without NEXT_PUBLIC_* env.

suggested_fix.approach: Provide NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL in CI/deploy build environments, preferably via repository/environment variables or explicit workflow env entries.
tests_needed: Run next build in a clean env with only the workflow-provided variables.

history.notes: Created during LYRA audit pass.
CI and deploy Build Web steps set NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL (and API URL) so next.config validation passes.
